englishDefinition: Making little or no noise.
To stop doing something or to leave a job.
To a fairly large degree; rather.
A short test of knowledge.
A small animal with long ears that hops on its back legs.
To compete with others in seeing who is fastest.
A device that receives sound broadcasts through the air.
A track made of metal rails on which trains run.
Water falling in drops from clouds in the sky.
A curved band of colors in the sky after rain when the sun shines.
Having a lot of rain.
To lift something up, or to take care of children as they grow.
An increase in the amount of money someone earns at work.
A line of mountains, or the limits between which something can change.
Happening quickly; fast.
Not happening often; unusual.
A small furry animal with a long tail, larger than a mouse.
Quite; to some degree.
To stretch out your hand to touch something, or to arrive at a place.
To look at words and understand their meaning.
Prepared; able to do something now.
Actually existing; not pretend or fake.
To suddenly understand something, or to make a dream come true.
Very; truly; in a way that is real.
The cause of something, or why someone does something.
To get something that someone gives or sends to you.
Happening or made a short time ago.
Information that has been written or saved for later use.
To save sound, video, or facts for later use.
To get well again after sickness or to get something back.
A shape with four straight sides where opposite sides are equal.
To use materials again instead of throwing them away.
The color of blood or a ripe tomato.
To talk about someone or something, or to look at a book for information.
A large kitchen appliance that keeps food cold.
To say no to something offered or asked.
To think of someone or something in a particular way.
A particular area of a country or the world.
To feel sad about something you did or did not do.
Happening at the same times in a fixed pattern.
To refuse to accept something.
Connected to something else; or a member of your family.
To rest and feel calm without worry or work.
A belief in a god or gods, with rules for how to live.
Faith in a god or higher power, often as part of a religion.
To keep something in your mind, or to bring it back to mind.
To make someone think of something they may have forgotten.
To pay money to use something, like a house or car, for a time.
To fix something that is broken or damaged.
To say or do something again.
To put a new thing or person in place of an old one.
To answer a question or letter.
To give an account of something to someone, in speech or writing.
A person whose job is to find out about events for newspapers or TV.
The official name of Taiwan.
To need or to demand something.
An arrangement made in advance to have a seat, room, or table.
To admire someone for their qualities or behavior.
Having the duty to take care of something or do a job.
A period of time when you stop working or moving.
To stop working or moving for a time so you feel less tired.
A place where people pay to eat meals.
A public toilet, especially in a building like a school or office.
The thing that happens because of an action or event.
To go back to a place, or to give something back.
An opinion or statement about a book, film, or product.
To study lessons again to help remember them.
To change a plan or text to make it better.
Small white or brown grains eaten as food, the main meal in many countries.
A food made from rice flour, often eaten at Chinese New Year.
Having a lot of money or goods.
To sit on or in a vehicle or animal as it moves.
Correct, or on the side opposite to left.
Done with or located on the right side.
A round band of metal worn on a finger as jewelry.
To make a clear loud sound, like a bell.
To make a bell make its sound, often as a signal.
To move up, or to increase in level.
A large flowing stream of water moving across the land to a lake or sea.
A wide, flat surface for cars and other vehicles to travel on.
To take money or things from someone using force or threats.
A machine that can do tasks like a person.
Hard stone, or a kind of loud music with strong rhythm.
To move gently from side to side or back and forth.
The part a person plays in a group or activity.
Something shaped by being wrapped around itself, like paper or bread.
To turn over and over while moving.
A boot with a single line of wheels under it for skating.
A boot with wheels under it for skating, often with two pairs of wheels.
The top covering of a building.
A part of a building with its own walls and doors.
The part of a plant that grows underground and takes in water.
A strong thick cord used for tying or pulling.
A flower with a sweet smell, often red, pink, or white, with thorns on its stem.
Having a shape like a circle or ball.
To use oars to make a boat move through water.
Belonging to or connected with a king or queen.
To move your hand back and forth on a surface while pressing down.
A strong stretchy material made from a tree or chemicals.
Not polite; speaking or acting in a way that hurts feelings.
